Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements reflect the core-cluster description in the review.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The review presents no new mechanistic findings for TRI6.

Source_Species.reason: TRI10 is listed in the characterized core TRI clusters of F. graminearum and F. sporotrichioides.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both species names are explicitly present.

Applied_Species.reason: The review discusses TRI genes across Fusarium trichothecene producers.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Generalization to Fusarium spp. is supported in context.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: TRI10 is part of the trichothecene biosynthetic gene cluster.

Regulator_Type.reason: The paper explicitly identifies TRI10 as one of two regulatory genes.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: As a regulatory TRI-cluster gene, it is linked to trichothecene biosynthesis.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper identifies TRI10 as a regulatory gene in the core TRI cluster involved in trichothecene biosynthesis.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by cluster characterization of the TRI locus.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three listed findings are consistent with the explicit cluster description.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The review does not discuss TRI10 mechanism in detail beyond cluster characterization.
